channel.title: RNS News — Equity & Markets Insight
channel.description: Summarised UK regulatory news with priority levels

item.title: Vodafone Group PLC — Vega is set to acquire e&'s entire 16.21% Vodafone stake and, via further instruments, build up to roughly 18.80% of share capital by year-end.
item.description: [HIGH] Vega's counterparty banks will purchase e&'s entire Vodafone holding — 3.94 billion shares , or 16.21% of share capital and 17.13% of voting rights — at £1.104792 per share for hedging purposes, following a binding agreement signed on 10 July . Vega has entered an additional financial instrument covering a further 2.74% of Vodafone's voting rights. Subject to regulatory approvals, the instruments

item.title: Rentokil Initial PLC — Rentokil Senior Independent Director John Pettigrew appointed as a non-executive director of SSE plc from 1 December 2026.
item.description: [LOW] John Pettigrew , Senior Independent Director of Rentokil Initial plc , has been appointed a non-executive director of SSE plc with effect from 1 December 2026 . Routine disclosure made under Listing Rule 6.4.9 with no impact on Rentokil's trading or guidance.

item.title: Greatland Resources Ltd — Greatland Resources will lodge its June Quarter 2026 Activities Report and host a shareholder webcast on Wednesday 29 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Greatland Resources Limited ( AIM:GGP , ASX:GGP ) will lodge its June Quarter 2026 Quarterly Activities Report with the ASX on Wednesday, 29 July 2026 . Accompanying webcast and Q&A at GMT 12:30am / BST 01:30am on 29 July 2026 ; registration and recording via the OpenBriefing link. Diary/administrative notice only — no financial figures, results or guidance disclosed in this announcement.

item.title: Kingfisher PLC — Kingfisher launches second £50 million tranche of its £300 million share buyback programme, running to no later than 15 September 2026.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Kingfisher has instructed Goldman Sachs International to buy back up to £50 million of shares for cancellation, commencing today and ending no later than 15 September 2026 . This is the second tranche of an overall £300 million programme first announced on 24 March 2026 , following completion of the first tranche of £75 million announced on 18 June 2026 . Maximum of 169,003,381 ordinary shares may

item.title: DFS Furniture PLC — DFS expects FY26 underlying PBT of around £45m, within its previously upgraded £43-50m guidance and up about £15m year on year, despite softer H2 demand.
item.description: [HIGH] DFS Furniture plc expects FY26 underlying PBT (PBTu(A)) of around £45m , within its previously upgraded £43-50m guidance and up around +£15m year on year, despite weaker H2 market demand. Profit underpinned by 2.7% revenue growth , gross margin expansion and cost discipline. Net bank debt cut to c£69m at 28 June (from £107m in FY25), with leverage down to 0.9x (FY25: 1.4x ). Group order intake sof

item.title: Foxtons Group PLC — Foxtons expects H1 2026 adjusted operating profit of about £8.5m, down from £12.3m, and guides full-year profit to £17m-£19m.
item.description: [HIGH] Foxtons Group plc expects H1 2026 adjusted operating profit of approximately £8.5m , down from £12.3m in H1 2025, against a strong prior-year comparator boosted by activity ahead of the March 2025 stamp duty deadline. Full-year 2026 Group adjusted operating profit guided to a range of £17m-£19m , with profit expected to be H2-weighted on Lettings seasonality and a normalisation in tenancy terminat

item.title: Dunelm Group PLC — Dunelm reports FY26 sales up 3.1% to £1,825m with full-year PBT expected in line with previous guidance and £210m consensus.
item.description: [HIGH] Dunelm Group plc FY26 trading update: full-year PBT expected in line with previous guidance and company-compiled consensus of £210m . Q4 sales up 2.9% to £428m , with digital participation up 3ppts to 45% ; growth achieved despite two weeks of exceptionally warm weather hitting store footfall. FY total sales up 3.1% to £1,825m , digital participation up 2ppts to 42% ; FY gross margin 52.5% , up 10

item.title: Frasers Group PLC — Frasers Group FY26 revenue rose 8.7% to £5,325.9m and retail profit 22.1% to £912.5m, but adjusted profit fell 4% and no FY27 guidance was given.
item.description: [HIGH] Frasers Group PLC FY26 (52 weeks to 26 April 2026 ): revenue up 8.7% to £5,325.9m , driven by 59.2% international revenue growth. Adjusted profit before tax ( APBT ) down 4.0% to £538.0m , hit by a £259.5m rise in fixed-asset impairments and higher interest costs; reported PBT up 38.9% to £527.8m on non-repeat derivative fair-value losses. Retail profit from trading up 22.1% to £912.5m ; UK Sports

item.title: Experian PLC — Experian posts strong Q1 FY27 with revenue up 10% at actual rates and 7% organically, reiterating unchanged full-year expectations.
item.description: [HIGH] Q1 FY27 (three months to 30 June 2026 ) revenue up 10% at actual exchange rates, 8% at constant currency and 7% organically, in line with expectations. Full-year expectations unchanged ; CEO Brian Cassin cites trusted data assets, scaled platforms and growing AI-enabled opportunities. North America organic revenue up 7% (total 8% ), led by Financial Services (Ascend, fraud, mortgage), Automotive (

item.title: Ocado Group PLC — Ocado Group publishes interim results for the 26 weeks ended 31 May 2026, with figures released via a separate PDF and a 9.30am analyst presentation.
item.description: [HIGH] Ocado Group plc has released interim results for the 26 weeks ended 31 May 2026 . The scraped announcement is a notice of publication only — the actual financial figures are in a linked RNS PDF and are not reproduced in this page text, so no revenue, profit, EPS or guidance figures can be quoted from the source. A results presentation for investors and analysts is scheduled for 9.30am on 16 July 2

item.title: Johnson Matthey PLC — Johnson Matthey appoints Joachim Rosenberg as an independent Non-Executive Director, effective from the conclusion of today's AGM.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Johnson Matthey appoints Joachim Rosenberg as an independent Non-Executive Director, effective from the conclusion of today's AGM . Rosenberg will also serve on the Audit, Nomination and Remuneration Committees . He brings international automotive experience, having spent twenty years at the Volvo Group — latterly as President of Volvo Energy from 2021 — plus nearly a decade at McKinsey & Company

item.title: BHP Group Ltd — BHP delivered record iron ore production of 265 Mt and ~2 Mt of copper in FY26, with FY26 unit costs expected within guidance and net debt of ~US$9bn.
item.description: [HIGH] BHP produced ~2 Mt (1,953 kt) of copper for the second consecutive year — down 3% on planned lower Escondida feed grade — and record iron ore of 265 Mt (up 1% ) in FY26 to 30 June 2026. FY26 unit costs expected at the bottom of guidance ranges for Escondida, Spence and Copper SA , within range at WAIO and towards the top end at BMA ; realised copper prices ~35% higher year-on-year. FY27 production

item.title: Kenmare Resources PLC — Kenmare trims 2026 ilmenite guidance to about 800,000 tonnes as WCP A commissioning problems cut Q2 HMC production 37% year-on-year.
item.description: [HIGH] Kenmare now expects 2026 ilmenite production of approximately 800,000 tonnes , down from previous guidance of more than 800,000 tonnes , after WCP A commissioning fell short of nameplate capacity. Q2 2026 HMC production of 225,000 tonnes, down 37% YoY , driven by a 29% fall in excavated ore grades as WCP A reaches the end of its Namalope mine path; ilmenite output 147,200 tonnes, down 40% YoY . Co

item.title: Trustpilot Group PLC — Trustpilot expects H1 bookings of $171m, up 22% (18% constant currency), driven by Enterprise and US momentum, and reaffirms full-year guidance.
item.description: [HIGH] Trustpilot Group plc expects H1 26 bookings of $171m (H1 25: $140m ), up 22% YoY and 18% at constant currency, led by Enterprise and US momentum. H1 26 revenue growth expected at 19% cc ; ARR from customers paying >$20k up 36% YoY , driven by AI Answer Engine Optimisation. LTM gross retention 87% (H1 25: 86%); net dollar retention 101% (H1 25: 103%) after annualising 2024-25 plan migrations. Perio

item.title: Crest Nicholson Holdings Ltd — Crest Nicholson swings to an H1 adjusted operating loss of £11.9m, guides FY26 profit to the lower half of £5m-£15m and extends its covenant waiver amid advanced lender talks.
item.description: [HIGH] Crest Nicholson Holdings plc posted an adjusted operating loss of £11.9m for the six months to 30 April 2026 , versus a £11.9m profit in HY25, on low completions and higher NRV charges. Adjusted gross margin fell to £13.9m from £35.4m , driven mainly by lower housing volume/mix ( -£12.7m ), land profit ( -£3.5m ) and increased apartment NRV provisions ( +£3.6m ). FY26 guidance lowered : adjusted p

item.title: Premier Foods PLC — Premier Foods reports Q1 branded revenue up 4.0% and total revenue up 2.7%, and remains on track to meet FY26/27 trading profit expectations.
item.description: [HIGH] Premier Foods Q1 (13 weeks to 27 June 2026 ): branded revenue up 4.0% , total revenue up 2.7% at constant currency. UK branded revenue up 3.8% , led by Sweet Treats (branded revenue up 6.6%); Mr Kipling among fastest-growing brands with sales up 9% . Grocery branded revenue up 3.0% (total up 1.3%); new categories up 16% helped by Cape Herb & Spice ; further market share gains in Grocery and Sweet

item.title: Helical PLC — Helical reports a strong start to the year with c.32,000 sq ft let at The Bower and a c.£12m B Share capital return proposed for approval today.
item.description: [HIGH] Trading update for the period 1 April to 15 July 2026 , released ahead of today's AGM : Matthew Bonning-Snook calls it a "strong start to the financial year" . The Bower, EC1 : today's 9,600 sq ft letting of the 12th floor to Audio Network takes total space let in the Period to c.32,000 sq ft , with re-gears on a further c.50,000 sq ft ; recent deals add £2.4m of contracted rent, lift occupancy to

item.title: Supermarket Income Reit PLC — Supermarket Income REIT has exchanged contracts to buy three supermarkets for £118m at a 6.9% net initial yield, completing September 2026.
item.description: [HIGH] Supermarket Income REIT plc has exchanged contracts to acquire a portfolio of three supermarkets for £118 million at an average net initial yield of 6.9% , with completion due in September 2026 . Stores are let on triple-net leases that are 100% inflation-linked and 100% investment grade , with a portfolio WAULT of 8 years and average rent of £34 per sq ft . Individual unexpired lease terms of 12,

item.title: Segro PLC — SEGRO signs three new leases across c.540,000 sq ft at SEGRO Park Coventry, adding £6m of rent to Volvo Group UK, DIRKS and GigaCloud.
item.description: [MEDIUM] SEGRO plc has signed three new leases across c.540,000 sq ft at SEGRO Park Coventry , including pre-lets to Volvo Group UK and DIRKS Consumer Logistics plus a speculative-unit lease to GigaCloud Technology . The lettings add an aggregate £6 million of new rent — already included in the £53 million of new headline rent reported in SEGRO's Trading Update on 8 July 2026 , so no incremental impact on

item.title: Standard Chartered PLC — Standard Chartered confirms Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director following receipt of regulatory approvals, effective immediately.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Manus Costello is appointed Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director of Standard Chartered PLC with immediate effect, following receipt of regulatory approvals. The appointment was first announced on 17 May 2026 ; today's release confirms completion. Routine directorate change with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: ICG PLC — All 22 resolutions passed at ICG's 2026 AGM; Stephen Welton and Rosemary Leith retired as Non-Executive Directors.
item.description: [LOW] All 22 resolutions put to ICG's 2026 AGM on 15 July 2026 were passed by the requisite majority. Highest dissent on the remuneration policy (10.23% against) and disapplication of pre-emption rights (11.53% against); W Rucker re-appointment drew 7.78% against. Stephen Welton and Rosemary Leith retired as Non-Executive Directors following the meeting; Leith had chaired the Risk Committee. Routine AGM

item.title: Paragon Banking Group PLC — Paragon Bank issues £500m of floating-rate covered bonds due September 2029 under its £5bn programme, priced at SONIA plus 0.50%.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Paragon Bank PLC , subsidiary of Paragon Banking Group PLC , issued £500,000,000 Series 2026-1 Floating Rate Covered Bonds due September 2029 under its £5 billion Covered Bond Programme . Priced at SONIA plus 0.50% and 1.7x subscribed by bank treasury and asset fund investors; secured against prime buy-to-let mortgages. Margin of 0.50% improves on the 0.60% paid at the inaugural March 2025 issuanc

item.title: Standard Chartered PLC — Standard Chartered confirms Manus Costello as Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director following regulatory approval, effective immediately.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Manus Costello appointed Group Chief Financial Officer and Executive Director of Standard Chartered PLC , with immediate effect after regulatory approvals were received. Confirms the appointment first announced on 17 May 2026 . Routine board completion — no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Aberdeen Group PLC — Aberdeen Asian Income Fund declares second quarterly interim dividend of 5.55p per share, an annualised yield of 6.30%.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Second quarterly interim dividend of 5.55p per share declared for the year ended 31 December 2026 . Equates to an annualised dividend yield of 6.30% , based on a share price of 335p on 30 June 2026 . Payable on 21 August 2026 to shareholders on the register at 24 July 2026 ; ex-dividend date 23 July 2026 . Dividend set at 1.5625% per quarter of NAV (approx 6.25% per annum ), based on NAV of 355.08

item.title: Segro PLC — SEGRO signs three new leases across c.540,000 sq ft at SEGRO Park Coventry, adding £6m of rent already booked in its H1 trading update.
item.description: [LOW] SEGRO plc has signed three new leases over c.540,000 sq ft at SEGRO Park Coventry , taking the site to almost 1.7m sq ft leased of a planned 3.7m sq ft. Deals comprise pre-lets to Volvo Group UK (91,000 sq ft build-to-suit) and DIRKS Consumer Logistics (306,000 sq ft), plus a speculative unit let to GigaCloud Technology (140,500 sq ft). Leases add £6m of new rent — already included in the £53m of

item.title: Londonmetric Property PLC — LondonMetric will report its half-year results for the six months to 30 September 2026 on Tuesday 24 November 2026.
item.description: [LOW] LondonMetric Property Plc will announce half year results for the six months ending 30 September 2026 on Tuesday 24 November 2026 . In-person analyst presentation at 08.30am on the day, with a live webcast at www.londonmetric.com/investors and an on-demand recording afterwards. Routine diary notice with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Franchise Brands PLC — Franchise Brands to host live investor presentation on its Interim Results via Investor Meet Company on 3 August 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Franchise Brands plc (AIM: FRAN) will hold a live presentation on its Interim Results via Investor Meet Company on 3 August 2026, 11:30 BST , led by Peter Molloy (CEO) and Neil Miller (CFO). Open to all existing and potential shareholders; questions can be submitted pre-event until 2 August 2026, 09:00 BST . Routine investor-engagement notice with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Intermediate Capital Group plc — ICG issues Q1 trading statement for the three months ended 30 June 2026, reporting AUM of $126bn, but the scraped text carries no performance figures.
item.description: [HIGH] Intermediate Capital Group plc (LSE: ICG ) published its Q1 trading statement for the three months ended 30 June 2026 . Reported total AUM of $126bn as a global alternative asset manager across Structured Capital and Secondaries, Debt and Real Assets. The scraped page contains only boilerplate business description and forward-looking-statement disclaimers — no fundraising, deployment, revenue or p

item.title: Ferrexpo PLC — Ferrexpo produced 963kt in Q2 with only one pellet line running and warns cash lasts only until early Q4 2026 absent a minimum US$100m equity raise.
item.description: [HIGH] Ferrexpo warns it has sufficient net accessible cash to operate only until early Q4 2026 , and remains in a "precarious financial position" reliant on a planned equity raise. Total Q2 2026 production of 963 thousand tonnes — 860kt of premium iron ore pellets and 103kt of Fe 67% concentrate — with only one pellet line in operation. Net cash less lease obligations of approximately US$21m at 30 June

item.title: Yellow Cake PLC — Yellow Cake swung to a USD419.5m annual profit as net asset value rose to USD2,109.6m (GBP6.33/share) on a 30% jump in the uranium spot price.
item.description: [HIGH] Profit after tax of USD419.5m for the year to 31 March 2026 , versus a loss of USD469.2m in 2025, driven mainly by a USD434.3m increase in the fair value of uranium holdings. Net asset value of USD2,109.6m (GBP6.33 per share) , up from USD1,414.4m (GBP5.05 per share) a year earlier. Uranium (U3O8) holdings rose to 23.11 million lb (2025: 21.68 million lb ), with the value of holdings up 39% to USD

item.title: Gore Street Energy Storage Fund PLC — Gore Street NAV fell to 74.9p per share from 102.8p, driven mainly by lower third-party merchant revenue forecasts, as revenue rose to £36.27m.
item.description: [HIGH] NAV dropped to 74.9pps ( £378.3m ) at 31 March 2026, from 102.8pps ( £519.3m ) a year earlier, with c. 68% of the decline attributed to lower independent merchant-revenue curves (-17.2p) plus a -1.8p Audit Committee adjustment. Revenue rose to £36.27m (FY25: £32.84m ) on a larger fleet, but average revenue fell to £7.32/MW/hr (FY25: £9.56/MW/hr ); operational EBITDA was broadly flat at £18.0m (FY2

item.title: Antofagasta PLC — Antofagasta's Q2 copper output of 142,000 tonnes was 1% lower quarter-on-quarter but full-year production and cost guidance are unchanged.
item.description: [HIGH] Q2 2026 copper production 142,000 tonnes , 1% lower quarter-on-quarter on reduced output at Antucoya ; ~ 7,000 tonnes processed at Los Pelambres deferred into H2 filtered production after concentrate pipeline maintenance. Year-to-date copper production 285,000 tonnes , 9% lower year-on-year on weaker output at Los Pelambres and Centinela ; quarterly output expected to rise sequentially through the

item.title: Supermarket Income Reit PLC — Supermarket Income REIT launches a £100m equity raise to fund a £216m advanced pipeline of nine UK grocery assets.
item.description: [HIGH] Supermarket Income REIT plc intends to raise approximately £100 million of gross proceeds via new ordinary shares to fund an advanced pipeline of nine grocery assets costing £216 million . Structure: an institutional accelerated bookbuild placing , a South African placing , and a UK retail offer via Retail Book; Goldman Sachs , Peel Hunt and Stifel are joint bookrunners, PSG Capital sole bookrunne

item.title: Rio Tinto PLC — Rio Tinto delivered 3% YoY copper equivalent production growth in H1 2026 and cut copper C1 net unit cost guidance to US 30-50c/lb.
item.description: [HIGH] Rio Tinto PLC reported 3% YoY growth in copper equivalent (CuEq) production for the first half of 2026, with overall production/sales guidance unchanged . Copper: Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up on track, delivering 31% YoY H1 growth; C1 net unit cost guidance cut to US 30-50c/lb (from US 65-75c/lb ) on higher gold prices and productivity gains. Iron ore: Q2 global iron ore sales 89Mt , up 5% YoY ; Q2 Pilbara

item.title: Hunting PLC — Hunting reports strong H1 momentum in Subsea and Perforating Systems, order book up to c.$387m, and reaffirms full-year EBITDA guidance of c.$145-$155m.
item.description: [HIGH] Hunting PLC H1 2026 trading update: Perforating Systems and Subsea traded ahead of expectations; full-year expectations unchanged . Period-end sales order book of c.$387m , up from the $358m 2025 year-end position; tender pipeline robust at c.$1.0bn . Full-year EBITDA guidance of c.$145-$155m maintained, in line with market expectations; year-end total cash/(borrowings) expected at c.$60-$65m . OC

item.title: Galliford Try Holdings PLC — Galliford Try expects a sixth consecutive year of revenue, profit and cash growth, with FY26 adjusted PBT at the top end of forecasts.
item.description: [HIGH] Galliford Try Holdings plc expects adjusted profit before tax at the top end of the £51.4m to £53.4m analysts' forecast range for the year ended 30 June 2026 . Sixth consecutive year of revenue, profit and cash growth expected; full-year revenue up circa 3% , with year-on-year margin progression towards the 2030 margin target of 4.0% . Year-end cash £258.8m (2025: £237.6m) and average month-end ca

item.title: Costain Group PLC — Costain wins three-year extension to its United Utilities Managed Service Provider contract, taking the partnership to ten years through the AMP8 cycle.
item.description: [HIGH] Costain awarded a three-year extension to its Managed Service Provider (MSP) contract with United Utilities , taking the partnership to ten years and through the remainder of the AMP8 regulatory cycle. Costain supports delivery of the AMP8 capital maintenance programme in the north west of England, with more than 120 professionals on the framework. United Utilities is investing £13.5bn in the regi

item.title: Workspace Group PLC — Workspace reports modest Q1 rent roll and occupancy gains, £12.6m of asset sales, and £138.4m of disposals against its £200m FY27 target.
item.description: [HIGH] Q1 to 30 June 2026 trading update: total portfolio rent roll up 1.8% (£2.3m) excluding disposals to £128.0m ; total occupancy up 0.3pp to 79.8% and rent per sq. ft. up 1.2% to £42.73 . Stabilised Portfolio occupancy up 0.6pp to 82.3% , rent per sq. ft. up 0.9% to £46.87 , lifting rent roll 1.9% (£2.1m) to £110.2m . 264 lettings and 111 renewals completed with total rental value of £11.9m (Q1 25/26

item.title: Topps Tiles PLC — Topps Tiles confirms it is consulting shareholders after resolution 13 passed with only 53.16% in favour at its January AGM.
item.description: [LOW] Topps Tiles Plc issues a statement under provision 4 of Section 1 of the UK Corporate Governance Code after its AGM on 15 January 2026 . More than 20% of votes were cast against resolution 13 , which passed with 53.16% in favour. Board says it has engaged with shareholders to understand the opposition and will continue to consult on the matter. Routine governance disclosure with no impact on tradi

item.title: Barratt Redrow PLC — Barratt Redrow delivered a solid FY26 with 17,667 completions and profit in line with expectations, and will return £400m to shareholders in FY27, mainly via buybacks.
item.description: [HIGH] Barratt Redrow plc FY26 trading update: adjusted profit before tax (before PPA adjustments) in line with market expectations ; annual results due 16 September 2026 . Total home completions up 5% to 17,667 (FY25: 16,826A/16,565R), at the upper end of guidance, including 566 JV and 3,774 affordable homes. Net private reservation rate 0.64 (FY25: 0.63A/0.64R); Q4 total private rate 0.75 (Q4 FY25: 0.7

item.title: Wizz Air Holdings PLC — Wizz Air appoints Jingyuan Sun as Chief Technical Officer, effective 1 October 2026, reporting to Group Managing Director Michael Delehant.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Wizz Air Holdings PLC appoints Jingyuan Sun as Chief Technical Officer , effective 1 October 2026 , reporting to Group Managing Director Michael Delehant . Role consolidates Technical Services, Engineering, Base Maintenance & Transitions and Technical Contracts & Supplier Management under a dedicated leadership structure; Sun joins the senior management team and is classed as a PDMR . Joins from S

item.title: Old Mutual Ltd — Old Mutual invites dissenting shareholders to a 29 July call after its remuneration policy and implementation report each missed the 75% JSE advisory-vote threshold.
item.description: [LOW] Old Mutual Limited is engaging dissenting shareholders after its Remuneration Policy (Resolution 5.1) and Remuneration Implementation Report (Resolution 5.2) each secured a majority but fell short of the 75% JSE advisory threshold at the 5 June 2026 AGM. Voting outcomes were 68.39% (policy) and 70.72% (implementation report), triggering engagement under paragraph 5.7(k) of the JSE Listings Require

item.title: Rio Tinto PLC — Rio Tinto reports copper equivalent production up 3% in H1 2026, with its highest first-half Pilbara iron ore output since the 2018 record.
item.description: [HIGH] Rio Tinto Q2/H1 2026 production update: group copper equivalent (CuEq) production up 3% year-on-year in the first half. Highest first-half Pilbara iron ore production since the 2018 record , driven by its productivity improvement programme. Copper: Oyu Tolgoi ramp-up on schedule, delivering more than 30% growth in the first half; aluminium business sustained strong performance. Simandou : SimFer m

item.title: Investec PLC — Investec's Share Incentive Plans made routine on-market purchases of Investec plc and Investec Limited shares between 8 and 10 July 2026.
item.description: [LOW] Investec plc and Investec Limited Share Incentive Plans 2021 acquired ordinary shares on-market to satisfy plan obligations to participants — a routine disclosure with no impact on trading or guidance. Investec plc shares: 100,000 at GBP 6.1040 (8 July), 100,000 at GBP 6.0921 (9 July) and 317,020 at GBP 6.0903 (10 July). Investec Limited shares acquired on 9-10 July at prices between ZAR 88.57 and

item.title: Compass Group PLC — Compass Group confirms the Sterling equivalent of its previously declared interim dividend is 19.0 pence per share.
item.description: [LOW] Compass Group PLC has set the Sterling equivalent of its interim dividend at 19.0 pence per ordinary share . Converts the 25.5 US cents per share interim dividend (declared 11 May 2026 ) at an exchange rate of US$1=£0.747 , payable 30 July 2026 . Routine FX-conversion disclosure with no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Babcock International Group PLC — Babcock admits £250m 5.125% notes due 2031 to trading on the London Stock Exchange main market under its £3bn EMTN programme.
item.description: [MEDIUM] Babcock International Group PLC admitted £250,000,000 5.125% Notes due 2031 to trading on the London Stock Exchange main market on 14 July 2026 . Notes issued under the group's £3,000,000,000 Euro Medium Term Note Programme (ISIN: XS3437585022), per final terms dated 10 July 2026 . Routine admission-to-trading notice tied to a debt issuance; no impact on trading or guidance.

item.title: Londonmetric Property PLC — LondonMetric grants further nil-cost LTIP options to CEO Andrew Jones (342,249 shares) and Martin McGann (179,680 shares) following AGM remuneration policy approval.
item.description: [LOW] Routine LTIP disclosure: nil cost options granted on 10 July 2026 under LondonMetric's Long Term Incentive Plan, following approval of the new remuneration policy at the 9 July 2026 AGM — no impact on trading or guidance. Andrew Jones awarded options over 342,249 shares; Martin McGann over 179,680 shares, in addition to shares granted on 1 June 2026 . Awards relate to the financial year ended 31 M

item.title: Greatland Resources Ltd — Greatland reports 437,500 options and 247,329 performance rights exercised by non-PDMR employees, satisfied by existing shares, plus 63,998 unvested rights lapsed.
item.description: [LOW] 437,500 vested options and 247,329 vested performance rights exercised by non-PDMR employees under the equity incentive plan. Exercises satisfied by transfer of existing shares from the employee share trust — no new shares issued and no dilution . 63,998 unvested performance rights held by former employees have lapsed on cessation of employment. Routine equity-plan administration with no impact on

item.title: Next PLC — Next 15 seeks AIM admission for 424,414 new ordinary shares issued for long-term incentive vesting, lifting the share count to 101,860,099.
item.description: [LOW] Next 15 Group plc has applied to admit 424,414 new ordinary shares (2.5p each) to AIM , with admission expected by 16 July 2026 . Shares issued as consideration for the vesting of long-term incentives relating to M. Booth & Associates LLC ; they will rank pari passu with existing shares. Total shares in issue will rise to 101,860,099 , with no shares held in treasury — a routine disclosure with no

item.title: Old Mutual Ltd — Old Mutual invites shareholders to a 29 July engagement call after its remuneration resolutions fell short of the JSE's 75% advisory threshold.
item.description: [LOW] Old Mutual Limited to hold a virtual shareholder engagement call at 15:30 SAST on Wednesday, 29 July 2026 on its remuneration policy and implementation report. At the AGM on 5 June 2026 , the Remuneration Policy (Ordinary Resolution 5.1) passed with 68.39% and the Remuneration Implementation Report (5.2) with 70.72% — both below the 75% advisory threshold under JSE Listings Requirement 5.7(k). Sha
